seoTitle: Warrawong vs Warregah Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Warrawong and Warregah Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Warrawong vs Warregah Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Warrawong and Warregah Island.
heading: Warrawong vs Warregah Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Warrawong, New South Wales and Warregah Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Warregah Island has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Warrawong (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Warrawong or Warregah Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Warrawong has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Warregah Island scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Warrawong scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Warregah Island scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Warrawong or Warregah Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Warregah Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
